```{r, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
knitr::kable(q22_tab, caption = "Q22: Training/Support Needs — Summary")

# ---- Plots ----
plot_data <- q22_tab %>%
  mutate(option_w = factor(str_wrap(as.character(option), 40),
                           levels = str_wrap(canon_levels, 40)))


# ---- 1) Sort and wrap long labels ----
plot_data <- q22_tab %>%
  mutate(
    option_w = fct_reorder(str_wrap(as.character(option), 40), Count)
  )

# ---- 1) Compute counts, percents, and labels ----
# ---- 1) Define denominator = number of unique respondents ----
denom <- 13  # or replace with n_distinct(faculty_analysis_data$.row) if dynamically computed

# ---- 2) Compute counts, percents, and labels ----
q22_faculty_tab <- plot_data %>%
  mutate(
    Percent = Count / denom,  # percentage of respondents
    Label   = paste0(Count, " (", round(Percent * 100, 1), "%)")
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Count))

# ---- 3) Horizontal bar chart (Counts + Percents) ----
p_q22_faculty_counts <- ggplot(q22_faculty_tab, aes(
  y = fct_reorder(option_w, Percent),
  x = Percent
)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#48C9B0", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +

  # Inline labels (Count + Percent)
  geom_text(
    aes(label = Label),
    hjust = -0.05,
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 3.6,
    color = "black"
  ) +

  # X-axis formatting (percent of faculty)
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, max(q22_faculty_tab$Percent) + 0.15),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))
  ) +

  # Titles & caption
  labs(
    title    = "Faculty Training and Support Needs for AI Integration",
    subtitle = "Multiple selections allowed; percentages based on faculty respondents (n = 13)",
    x        = "Percentage of Faculty",
    y        = "Training or Support Area",
    caption  = "Percentages represent proportion of faculty (not responses)."
  ) +

  # Publication theme (consistent with Q18–Q20)
  theme_publication() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10.5, lineheight = 1.1, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray30"),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, hjust = 0, color = "gray40"),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 100, 35, 15)   # extra right margin for long labels
  )

# ---- 4) Save high-resolution figure ----
if (!dir.exists("Faculty_figures")) dir.create("Faculty_figures", recursive = TRUE)

ggsave(
  filename = "Faculty_figures/Q22_faculty_training_needs.png",
  plot = p_q22_faculty_counts,
  dpi = 700,
  width = 9.0,
  height = 5.5,
  units = "in"
)

# ---- 5) Display plot & summary table ----
print(p_q22_faculty_counts)

knitr::kable(
  q22_faculty_tab %>%
    rename(`Training/Support Area` = option_w) %>%
    select(`Training/Support Area`, Count, Percent),
  caption = "Q22: Faculty Training and Support Needs (Percent of Faculty, n = 13)"
)
```


## Q23: Do you believe your institution provides sufficient guidance and resources for using AI in teaching?


```{r, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

faculty_analysis_data <- if (exists("faculty_data_consented")) faculty_data_consented else faculty_data

q23_col <- dplyr::case_when(
  "Q23" %in% names(faculty_analysis_data) ~ "Q23",
  "q23" %in% names(faculty_analysis_data) ~ "q23",
  TRUE ~ NA_character_
)
stopifnot(!is.na(q23_col))

canon_levels <- c("Yes", "No", "Somewhat")

faculty_analysis_data <- faculty_analysis_data %>%
  mutate(
    q23_raw = .data[[q23_col]],
    q23_std = case_when(
      is.numeric(q23_raw) & q23_raw == 1 ~ "Yes",
      is.numeric(q23_raw) & q23_raw == 2 ~ "No",
      is.numeric(q23_raw) & q23_raw == 3 ~ "Somewhat",
      str_detect(as.character(q23_raw), regex("^yes$", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Yes",
      str_detect(as.character(q23_raw), regex("^no$",  ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "No",
      str_detect(as.character(q23_raw), regex("somewhat", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Somewhat",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ) |> factor(levels = canon_levels)
  )

# ---- Summary table ----
q23_ans <- faculty_analysis_data %>% filter(!is.na(q23_std))
denom   <- nrow(q23_ans)

q23_tab <- q23_ans %>%
  count(q23_std, name = "Count") %>%
  tidyr::complete(q23_std = factor(canon_levels, levels = canon_levels),
                  fill = list(Count = 0)) %>%
  mutate(Percent = round(100 * Count / pmax(denom, 1), 1),
         q23_std = factor(q23_std, levels = canon_levels))

knitr::kable(q23_tab,
             col.names = c("Response", "Count", "Percent"),
             caption   = "Q23: Does your institution provide sufficient AI guidance/resources? — Summary")

q23_tab <-  q23_ans  %>%
  filter(!is.na(q23_std) & q23_std != "") %>%
  count(q23_std, name = "Count") %>%
  mutate(
    Percent = Count / sum(Count),
    Label   = paste0(Count, " (", round(Percent * 100, 1), "%)")
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Percent))

n_valid <- sum(q23_tab$Count)

# ---- 2) Prepare plot data with wrapped labels ----
plot_data <- q23_tab %>%
  mutate(Response = str_wrap(as.character(q23_std), 44))

# ---- 3) Horizontal bar chart (publication style) ----
p_q23 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(
  y = fct_reorder(Response, Percent),
  x = Percent
)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#48C9B0", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +

  # Inline labels (counts + percents)
  geom_text(
    aes(label = Label),
    hjust = -0.05,
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 3.6,
    color = "black"
  ) +

  # X-axis (percent scale)
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, max(plot_data$Percent) + 0.1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))
  ) +

  # Titles & caption
  labs(
    title    = "Q23: Do you believe your institution provides \nsufficient guidance and resources for using AI in teaching?",
    subtitle = "Single response per faculty; percentages add to 100%",
    x        = "Percentage of Faculty",
    y        = "Response",
    caption  = paste0("n = ", n_valid, " valid faculty responses.")
  ) +

  # Publication-style theme
  theme_publication() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 10.5, lineheight = 1.1),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 10.5),
    plot.title   = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle= element_text(size = 11),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.margin  = margin(10, 100, 35, 15)   # extra right margin for labels
  )

# ---- 4) Save high-resolution figure ----
if (!dir.exists("Faculty_figures")) dir.create("Faculty_figures", recursive = TRUE)

ggsave(
  filename = "Faculty_figures/Q23_Institutional_Guidance.png",
  plot = p_q23,
  dpi = 700,
  width = 9.0,
  height = 5.5,
  units = "in"
)

# ---- 5) Display plot & summary table ----
print(p_q23)
knitr::kable(
  q23_tab %>% rename(Response = q23_std),
  caption = paste0("Q23: Institutional Guidance/Resources (n = ", n_valid, ")")
)
```
